spec_match.rationale: The page now distinguishes current Chrome's behavior-token syntax from the explainer's grid intent, keeps unsupported grid tokens out of the live native attribute, and demonstrates logical block/inline routing across LTR, RTL, and vertical writing modes.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the writing-mode buttons, strict vertical-only toggle, visible intent string, key-event log, and color selection path all update in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and note now explain that this is a grid-routing polyfill using data-focusgroup-intent because Chrome Canary rejects the native grid token unless the grid behavior flag is enabled.

edge_cases.rationale: Writing-mode remapping, RTL, wrap, no-wrap, Home, End, and strict vertical-only are covered; PageUp/PageDown and irregular final rows are still not demonstrated.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5637601087193088 and the Open UI explainer.

design_system.rationale: Variables drive the page chrome and the new note/readout; grid cell colors are direct hsl swatches because the colors are the content being selected.

openQuestions.title: Add Page Up / Page Down keymap
openQuestions.detail: Large grids need page-step navigation. Wire PageUp/PageDown to a configurable row-step, such as four rows, so the demo covers more than per-cell arrows.

openQuestions.title: Show a non-rectangular grid
openQuestions.detail: Real colour pickers can have an irregular last row. Add a 'remove last 3 cells' toggle so wrap and end behaviour at an incomplete row is visible.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome Canary through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v146/focusgroup/grid-navigation/: vertical-rl ArrowRight wrapped from r0c0 to r0c7, ArrowLeft returned to r0c0, strict vertical-only ignored horizontal arrows, and console/network were clean. The previous writing-mode and polyfill-disclosure questions are resolved.
